questions.text: A traffic signal board is an equilateral triangle. If its perimeter is 180 cm, its area is:
questions.explanation: Side = 180/3 = 60 cm. Area of equilateral triangle = (√3/4)a² = (√3/4)(60)² = 900√3 cm²

questions.text: For a triangle with sides 3cm, 4cm, 5cm, the area is 6 cm².
questions.explanation: This is a right triangle (3-4-5). Area = (1/2) × 3 × 4 = 6 cm². Or using Heron's formula: s=6, Area=√[6(3)(2)(1)]=6 cm²

questions.text: When do we use Heron's formula?
questions.correctAnswer: When all three sides of a triangle are known but height is not known.
questions.explanation: Heron's formula is useful when we know the three sides but cannot easily find the height.

questions.text: If sides of a triangle are doubled, its area becomes ________ times.
questions.explanation: Area is proportional to square of sides. If sides are doubled (2x), area becomes 2² = 4 times.
